arms.predicted: seed_upper (bracket UPPER endpoint) achieves gap_throughput_rel <= seed_lower on every dev scenario, with gap_rel near zero (<= ~0.005) on all six, at exactly 1 oracle call. Improvement is largest where occupancy ratio M_truth/M_ITL is largest.
arms.observed: CONFIRMED on all six scenarios at 1 call. seed_upper gap_rel: baseline=0.000125, itl-only=0.002729, ttft-only=0.000886, unbounded=0.0000, alpha-low=6.74e-05, alpha-high=9.37e-05. All <= 0.0027 (well below ~0.005 threshold). seed_upper <= seed_lower on every scenario: baseline 0.000125 vs 0.2345, itl-only 0.002729 vs 0.4831, ttft-only 0.000886 vs 0.000886 (equal, as expected since both endpoints coincide), unbounded 0.0 vs 0.0 (equal, degenerate), alpha-low 6.74e-05 vs 0.1011, alpha-high 9.37e-05 vs 0.7580. The improvement is largest for alpha-high (ratio M_truth/M_ITL ~ 17/6=2.83, delta_gap=0.7579) and itl-only (17/8=2.13, delta_gap=0.4804), consistent with the occupancy-ratio ordering prediction.
arms.diagnostic_note: The f-plateau extends from well below the upper endpoint to M_MAX on all six scenarios under nc=1. The upper endpoint lands on the plateau in every case, confirming RP-2: M_ITL is a strict lower bound on the plateau onset M*, and the occupancy gap pushes M* above M_ITL.

arms.predicted: seed_lower (LOWER endpoint M_ITL) shows materially larger gap_rel than seed_upper on the four ITL-binding scenarios (baseline, itl-only, alpha-low, alpha-high), while ttft-only and unbounded are unchanged.
arms.observed: CONFIRMED exactly as predicted. ITL-binding scenarios: baseline gap_rel(seed_lower)=0.2345 vs 0.000125, itl-only=0.4831 vs 0.002729, alpha-low=0.1011 vs 6.74e-05, alpha-high=0.7580 vs 9.37e-05 — all materially inflated. ttft-only: both 0.000886 (equal, both seeds reduce to M_TPF=70 in the ttft-only cell). unbounded: both 0.0 (equal, both reduce to M_MAX=256). The ablation is specific to the itl-or-crossover cell where the occupancy gap lives, exactly as the mechanism predicts.
arms.diagnostic_note: M_truth/M_ITL ratios: baseline=69/40=1.73, itl-only=17/8=2.13, alpha-low=170/107=1.59, alpha-high=17/6=2.83. All well above 1, confirming M_ITL is strictly below the plateau onset for all four scenarios. The upper endpoint (not the lower) is the load-bearing component of seed_upper.

arms.predicted: naive_max (return M_MAX=256, no formula) matches seed_upper's gap_throughput_rel to within float noise on all six scenarios. The throughput axis cannot discriminate a precise formula seed from constant M_MAX on the nc=1 dev set.
arms.observed: CONFIRMED. naive_max gap_rel: baseline=0.0001253, itl-only=0.002729, ttft-only=8.84e-08, unbounded=0.0, alpha-low=6.74e-05, alpha-high=9.70e-05. Max absolute difference |gap_rel(seed_upper) - gap_rel(naive_max)| = 0.000886 (ttft-only, where seed_upper returns M_TPF=70 and naive_max returns 256 — both on the plateau, with ttft-only's plateau starting below 70 and extending past 256). All differences are within ~0.001, well within float noise threshold ~0.005. On the nc=1 dev set, both strategies are throughput-equivalent.
arms.diagnostic_note: The f-plateau's 99% onset is below M_TPF=70 for ttft-only (truth M_truth=92, but plateau already flat at 70), and extends to M=256 on all six scenarios. Under nc=1, delta is constant and S(B)=1000B/tau(B) is concave-increasing (RP-3), so f(M) never declines within [1,256]. The throughput benefit of formula precision requires nc>1 (iter-5 benchmark), where tau goes super-linear and S(B) peaks-and-declines.

arms.predicted: For ttft-only, M_truth=92 lies above U=M_TPF=70 (bracket fails to contain M_truth), yet seed_upper's gap_rel at U is near zero (<= ~0.005). Throughput-optimality is robust to the bracket missing the argmax.
arms.observed: CONFIRMED. ttft-only: seed_upper chooses M=70 (M_TPF), M_truth=92, gap_rel=0.000886 < 0.005. The bracket [M_ITL=95 wait, M_TPF=70] misses M_truth=92 (70 < 92 < 95), yet evaluating at 70 yields f(70) ~ f_truth because the plateau onset is below 70 for ttft-only. For all other five scenarios the bracket contains M_truth. RP-5 upper-bounds the TTFT-CONSTRAINT crossing, not the throughput argmax.
arms.diagnostic_note: RP-5 is corrected: M_TPF upper-bounds the TTFT-constraint-binding M (where ttft_prefill(B)<=targetTTFT), not the throughput argmax. The plateau onset M* can be (and is for ttft-only) below M_TPF. The bracket is a constraint-crossing bracket; throughput only requires landing past the plateau onset.

discrepancy_analysis: All four arms confirmed with zero directional errors. The central reframe (upper endpoint is the throughput-optimal one-call seed) is validated by the live oracle. The occupancy gap (RP-2) is confirmed: M_truth/M_ITL spans 1.59-2.83 on ITL-binding scenarios, M_ITL is strictly on the rising part of f(M). The nc=1 plateau control (h-control-negative) confirms that naive_max ties seed_upper on the dev set — throughput discrimination requires the nc>1 benchmark (iter-5). The bracket robustness result (h-robustness) corrects RP-5: M_TPF is a constraint-crossing upper bound, not a throughput-argmax upper bound. No seeds produced infeasible confirmatory calls (throughput>0 on all scenarios), so the iter-4 queue-infeasibility guard is precautionary, not urgent on the dev set.
